topic: A student is accused of plagiarism
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the library hummed, a grating sound that seemed to amplify the frantic thumping in my chest. I stared at the printed accusation, the damning words swimming before my eyes. Every line felt sharp, like shards of glass digging into my skin. My hands, slick with nervous sweat, trembled as I reread the cited passages. A cold wash spread through my limbs, making it hard to focus on the words. I wanted to crumple the paper, to disappear into the stacks of forgotten books, but my feet remained rooted to the floor. The silence of the vast reading room pressed in, heavy and suffocating, punctuated only by the frantic rhythm of my own breathing.

[story 2]

"How could you?" The professor's voice, calm and utterly devoid of warmth, echoed in the small, sterile office. I kept my gaze fixed on the polished wood of the desk, refusing to meet his eyes. A tightness constricted my throat, making swallowing a painful effort. My knuckles were white where I gripped the edge of my notebook. Internally, a tidal wave of panic was rising, threatening to swamp me entirely. I could feel a strange, hollow ache in my stomach, and a sudden urge to flee, to bolt out of the room and never look back. Every sound—the rustle of paper, the click of the door closing—felt magnified, each one an indictment.

[story 3]

I walked across the quad, the autumn air biting at my exposed skin. Each step felt weighted, laborious. The campus architecture seemed to lean in, judgmental and tall. My mind was a chaotic mess, a whirlwind of accusations and self-doubt. I kept running the scenario over and over in my head: the presentation, the late nights, the sheer exhaustion that had led to this precipice. A tremor ran through my hands, and I quickly shoved them deep into my pockets, trying to quell the involuntary shaking. When I finally reached the administrative building, my legs felt unsteady, and I could only manage a shallow, ragged breath before approaching the intimidating glass doors.

My mind was a chaotic mess, a whirlwind of accusations and self-doubt.
